Drifting inches above dark water, her ghostly form reflected as shimmering ripples spreading outward beneath her.
Splitting into three separate orbs of light that float apart, then slowly spiral back together into her humanoid shape.
Reaching toward a distant lantern, her arm elongating into a thin tendril of light that stretches impossibly far.
Flickering rapidly between visible and invisible, each flash leaving translucent afterimages suspended in the fog.
Rising from a bog pool's surface, her body assembling from dozens of firefly-like points coalescing upward.
Trailing fingers through thick mist, carving glowing lines that hang luminous in the air before fading.
Hovering at a path's fork, her light pulsing brighter toward the treacherous direction, dimmer toward safety.
Passing through an ancient tree trunk, her light diffusing through bark and emerging whole on the far side.
Dancing alone in a clearing, her movements leaving long-exposure light trails spiraling through the darkness.
Crouching at water's edge, her reflection glowing far brighter than her actual form in the black pool.
Dimming to near-extinction then flaring brilliant, each pulse sending waves of light rippling through surrounding fog.
Drifting backward into deeper mist, her outline dissolving until only two bright eyes remain visible.
Beckoning with one hand, her fingers separating into five individual lights that curl and uncurl invitingly.
Swirling in place like a vortex, her body breaking into spiraling ribbons of light and reforming continuously.
Skimming across the marsh surface, leaving a trail of phosphorescent ripples that glow green in her wake.
Fragmenting into a cloud of sparks that drift upward like embers before collapsing back into her form.
Leaning over stagnant water, her face multiplying into dozens of glowing reflections across the rippled surface.
Phasing between solid and translucent, her core brightening as her edges blur into formless radiance.
Ascending slowly through hanging willow branches, her light casting moving shadows that twist like grasping hands.
Condensing from diffuse fog into her shape, drawing surrounding mist inward like breath pulled into lungs.
Extending both arms wide, her body stretching into a horizontal bar of light before snapping back.
Circling a rotted signpost, leaving a helical trail of light that winds around the wood and fades.
Sinking waist-deep into marsh water, her lower half dissolving into glowing tendrils beneath the surface.
Pulsing arrhythmically, her brightness surging and fading like a failing heartbeat struggling against darkness.
Drifting in a slow figure-eight pattern, her path marked by lingering afterglow that traces her movement.
